headline: TE Connectivity (TEL) Is Down 12.8% After Record Q2 Orders And Higher AI Revenue Outlook - Has The Bull Case Changed?
summary: Earlier this week, TE Connectivity reported past second-quarter 2026 results with sales of about US$4,744 million and net income of US$855 million, and issued guidance for third-quarter 2026 sales of approximately US$5,000 million and GAAP diluted EPS from continuing operations of about US$2.44. The company also highlighted record orders and stronger AI-related revenue expectations, indicating that demand for its connectivity solutions in data networks, energy, and aerospace/defense is...
